categories.title: New Orleans Swamp Tours
categories.description: Compare swamp tours, airboat rides, and bayou experiences that pair well with a New Orleans city trip.
categories.intro: Swamp tours are one of the most popular ways visitors add Louisiana landscape and wildlife to a New Orleans stay. They work best as a half-day or day-trip experience, especially if you want a strong contrast to the French Quarter, food, and music parts of the trip.
bullets: Best for first-time visitors who want a classic Louisiana experience beyond the city center
Often easiest to do in the morning or as a dedicated half-day block
Works well alongside food, music, and neighborhood-focused city days
Good fit for families, mixed-age groups, and visitors who want scenery and wildlife

intents.label: Airboat swamp tours
intents.description: Faster-paced rides for visitors who want more action on the water.

intents.label: Bayou tours
intents.description: A slower way to see marsh scenery, wildlife, and surrounding wetlands.

intents.label: Family-friendly swamp tours
intents.query: family friendly swamp tour new orleans
intents.description: Useful for visitors balancing city highlights with easy outdoor time.

intents.label: Half-day swamp tours
intents.query: new orleans half day swamp tour
intents.description: The easiest option for fitting a swamp experience into a short trip.

categories.title: New Orleans Ghost Tours
categories.description: Find ghost tours, haunted history walks, and darker New Orleans night experiences that fit naturally with French Quarter plans.
categories.intro: Ghost tours work best when the trip needs a clear night lane instead of generic Bourbon Street wandering. This page should help a visitor choose between French Quarter storytelling, haunted-history walks, cemetery lore, and darker tours that lean into voodoo, legends, and old New Orleans atmosphere.
categories.inventoryTitle: Top New Orleans Ghost Tours Right Now
bullets: Best fit for first-timers who want a defined French Quarter night instead of improvising
Useful when the goal is atmosphere, local storytelling, and darker city history instead of generic nightlife
Easy to pair with dinner, cocktails, or a later music stop without overbuilding the evening
Strong option for short stays because the tours fit cleanly into a single night block

intents.label: French Quarter ghost walks
intents.query: french quarter ghost walk new orleans
intents.description: The cleanest first lane for visitors who want classic haunted storytelling in the most recognizable neighborhood.

intents.label: Haunted history tours
intents.description: Best when the priority is deeper local context instead of a novelty-heavy scare format.

intents.label: Cemetery night tours
intents.description: Useful for travelers who want a darker route with more lore, mood, and old-city atmosphere.

intents.label: Voodoo and dark-history experiences
intents.description: A stronger fit for visitors who want occult history, legends, and a less generic ghost-tour angle.

categories.title: New Orleans Food Tours
categories.description: Discover food tours, tasting walks, and culinary experiences that help visitors choose a real New Orleans eating lane instead of one generic meal stop.
categories.intro: Food tours work best when the page helps visitors choose the kind of eating block they actually want: classic French Quarter Creole, a beignet-and-coffee crawl, a richer brunch lane, or a cooking-class experience that turns the meal into the main event.
categories.inventoryTitle: Top New Orleans Food Tours Right Now
bullets: Best for travelers who want structure instead of guessing where to eat first
Easy to pair with the French Quarter, Garden District, or a shorter weekend itinerary
Strong option for first-day orientation because the best food tours also teach the city
Useful when the goal is to combine history, walking, and a real meal block in one booking

intents.description: The cleanest first lane for visitors who want classic dishes, local context, and easy Quarter routing.

intents.label: Beignet and coffee crawls

categories.title: New Orleans Jazz and Music Tours
categories.description: Find jazz-focused tours, live-music walks, and heritage experiences that help visitors plan around real New Orleans music neighborhoods and venues.
categories.intro: Jazz tours work best when the trip needs more than generic nightlife. This page should help a visitor choose between French Quarter heritage, Frenchmen Street club energy, Preservation Hall-style history, and music-first experiences that shape the whole night.
bullets: Best fit for music-first trips and first-time visitors who want more than a random bar crawl
Useful for planning around Frenchmen Street, Preservation Hall, and classic Quarter music history
Easy to pair with dinner, cabaret, or a later-night club block instead of wandering blind
Strong option when the goal is context plus live music, not just nightlife volume
